title: x360 :: 5th-30th Apr: 572-616 in siege of Falluja
pages_or_sections: 'How this particular number was arrived at'
strengths: Most transparent publicly accessible reconstruction for April civilian deaths; explicitly discusses alternative Iraqi Health Ministry totals and shows allocation logic.
limitations: Not a named-victim registry; civilian range is inferred from mixed local totals using assumptions about adult-male civilian deaths and fighter share.
notes: Core April civilian numerator source. Also supplies the local total of 800 deaths from which a case-specific insurgent remainder can be derived.

title: x453 :: Nov-Dec 2004: 581-670 in nine neighbourhoods of Falluja
pages_or_sections: 'How this particular number was arrived at'
date_scope: 2004-11-08 to 2004-11-30 (with some possible December spillover assigned to November)
strengths: Most explicit public method note for the November assault's civilian deaths; records overlap risk and explains why estimate is provisional.
limitations: Incomplete by its own terms: rubble recoveries only, only 9 of 27 neighbourhoods, excludes many earlier burials and open-area recoveries.
notes: Core November/December civilian numerator source, but should be read as a conservative floor-range, not a complete census.

title: The Battle for Fallujah
author_or_org: Chief Warrant Officer 4 Timothy S. McWilliams, with Nicholas J. Schlosser
date_scope: Primarily 2004-11 to 2004-12, with introductory context on April
strengths: Best single official source for second-battle coalition casualties, force composition, and doctrinal narrative.
limitations: Official-history perspective; civilian harms are not the focus; some force/population claims are estimates rather than audited counts.
notes: Used mainly for second-battle military numerators and theater description.

title: Battle of Fallujah Staff Ride Read-Ahead Guidance
author_or_org: Army University Press / Educational Services, Virtual Staff Ride Program
strengths: Useful cross-battle synthesis with orders of battle, force sizes, and battle-end casualty summaries.
limitations: Still a military-institutional account; some civilian-presence statements (e.g., less than 500 remaining) are unattributed estimates.
notes: Key denominator source for second battle and a useful April U.S. KIA anchor.

title: Operation Vigilant Resolve: The First Battle of Al Fallujah
source_type: history magazine article / excerpt from official history
strengths: Detailed first-battle task-organization and checkpoint/cordon narrative with explicit mention of population and Iraqi-force desertion.
limitations: Retrospective military narrative rather than a civilian-harm study; no direct civilian mortality reconstruction.
notes: Used mainly to bound April theater population and direct military deployment.

title: Why America Has Waged a Losing Battle on Fallouja
author_or_org: Alissa J. Rubin and Doyle McManus
strengths: Provides explicit troop-count progression for the April offensive from planning to reinforcement.
limitations: Not a primary operational log; troop strength still needs modeling into average exposure.
notes: Useful for April military denominator scenarios.

title: Thousands in Fallouja Flee; Council Totters
strengths: Contemporaneous numeric claims on troop reinforcement and a large same-week displacement datapoint.
limitations: News reporting; displacement figure is a contemporaneous estimate, not an audited register.
notes: Used for April troop ramp-up and civilian outflow.

title: IBC Extracted Falluja News - April 09
strengths: Preserves contemporaneous reporting on who was allowed to leave and how outflow operated during pauses.
limitations: Archive of secondary reporting rather than a standalone primary source.
notes: Used qualitatively to support the civilian-presence adjustment in April.

title: IBC Extracted Falluja News - April 12
strengths: Contemporaneous city-size anchor and report of four battalions around the city.
limitations: Secondary extracted journalism, not an administrative population count.
notes: Useful as a denominator anchor, not as a core mortality source.

title: IBC Extracted Falluja News - April 29
strengths: Late-April contemporaneous estimate of insurgent numbers and city population.
limitations: Secondary archive of AP reporting; not a formal force census.
notes: Used mainly for April insurgent-strength context.

title: Iraqi, U.S. Troops Begin 'Al Fajr' Operation in Fallujah
author_or_org: Jim Garamone / American Forces Press Service (archived at DVIDS)
strengths: Contemporaneous official estimate of civilians and insurgents remaining before the assault.
limitations: Official messaging source; archived page was reposted later on DVIDS, so dating requires care.
notes: Used as one of several competing denominator anchors for the second battle.

title: Faces of Battle: The Insurgents in Falluja
pages_or_sections: 'The Setting and the Odds' and subsequent analysis
strengths: Useful independent synthesis of force ratio, city size, displacement share, and a lower insurgent-killed estimate.
limitations: Think-tank analysis relying heavily on coalition reporting; not a victim registry.
notes: Important counterweight to the higher official 2,000-insurgent-killed figure.

title: US troops ban men from entering, leaving Fallujah
strengths: Contemporaneous report that quantifies remaining civilian share and documents age-sex exit restrictions.
limitations: News report relaying military estimates; not an audited population count.
notes: Used to justify why civilian flight should not be modeled as complete for all population groups.

title: IRAQ: 800 Civilians Feared Dead in Fallujah
strengths: One of the few contemporaneous humanitarian-source reports suggesting a much higher civilian toll and a large trapped population.
limitations: Anonymous Red Cross official; methodology opaque; not suitable as sole core numerator.
notes: Used as contextual evidence that IBC x453 is likely incomplete on the high side.
